absorption_notes: Take on empty stomach 30-60 min before food. Calcium, iron, and antacids reduce absorption.
Can be taken with or without food. Avoid potassium supplements.
Well absorbed regardless of food. Grapefruit may increase levels.
Take with meals to reduce GI side effects. Avoid excessive alcohol.
Can be taken any time but evening preferred. Avoid grapefruit.
Take with food to enhance absorption. Do not stop abruptly.
Take 30-60 min before meals for best acid suppression.
Can be taken with or without food. Avoid potassium-rich foods in excess.
Take in the evening. Avoid grapefruit juice—increases risk of rhabdomyolysis.
Low-dose: take with food to reduce GI irritation. Enteric-coated forms should not be crushed.
Take with food or milk to reduce stomach irritation.
Can be taken with or without food. Avoid alcohol—hepatotoxicity risk.
Can be taken with or without food. Complete full course.
Take 1 hour before or 2 hours after meals for best absorption.
Avoid dairy, calcium, iron, and antacids within 2 hours. Drink plenty of water.
Take with food and full glass of water. Avoid lying down for 30 min after. Avoid dairy.
Can be taken with or without food. Complete full course.
Take with food and a full glass of water to reduce esophageal irritation.
Take with food to reduce nausea. Absolutely avoid alcohol during and 48h after.
Can be taken with or without food. Many drug interactions—check CYP2C9/CYP3A4.
Take with food to increase bioavailability. Avoid alcohol and St. John's Wort.
Can be taken with or without food. Consistent timing preferred.
Can be taken with or without food. Very long half-life.
Can be taken with or without food. Max 40mg/day due to QT prolongation.
Take with food to reduce nausea. Do not stop abruptly—taper required.
Take with food. Extended-release: swallow whole, do not crush.
Do not crush or chew—enteric coated. Can take with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food. Avoid alcohol—seizure risk.
Take with food to reduce dizziness. Often used as sleep aid at low doses.
Take at bedtime due to sedation. May increase appetite.
Can be taken with or without food. Avoid alcohol and grapefruit.
Can be taken with or without food. Avoid alcohol.
Can be taken with or without food. Very long half-life with active metabolites.
Can be taken with or without food. Do not stop abruptly.
Take on empty stomach immediately before bed. Food delays absorption.
Can be taken with or without food. Dose-dependent absorption saturation.
Can be taken with or without food. Linear absorption unlike gabapentin.
Can be taken with or without food. Slow titration required—risk of SJS.
Take with food to reduce GI upset. Monitor liver function and blood levels.
Take with food. Autoinducer of CYP3A4—many drug interactions.
Take with food. Maintain consistent sodium and fluid intake. Narrow therapeutic index.
Can be taken with or without food. Causes sedation—bedtime preferred.
Can be taken with or without food. Monitor metabolic parameters.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food. Very long half-life.
Take with food. Take in morning to avoid nighttime urination.
Take on empty stomach for faster action. Morning dose preferred.
Take with food to increase absorption. Avoid potassium supplements.
Take at consistent time daily. Maintain consistent vitamin K intake. Many food/drug interactions.
Can be taken with or without food. Avoid omeprazole—reduces activation.
15mg and 20mg doses MUST be taken with food for adequate absorption.
Can be taken with or without food.
Take with breakfast or first main meal. Risk of hypoglycemia if meal is skipped.
Take 30 minutes before meals for best effect.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food. Increases urinary glucose excretion.
Can be taken with or without food. Morning dose preferred.
Inject at same time daily. Do not mix with other insulins.
Inject at any time of day, with or without meals. Consistent timing preferred.
Oral form: take on empty stomach with sip of water, wait 30 min before eating.
Take 30 minutes before a meal. Swallow whole.
Take at least 1 hour before meals. Do not crush or chew.
Take before meals.
Take 15-60 min before meals or at bedtime.
Take before meals. Note: many formulations withdrawn due to NDMA concerns.
Can be taken with or without food. ODT form dissolves on tongue.
Take 15-30 minutes before meals.
Take after each loose stool. Max 16mg/day.
Take at bedtime for morning effect. Do not take with milk or antacids.
Can be mixed with water or juice. May cause bloating initially.
Take in the evening. Can be taken with or without food.
Inhaler: use as needed. Oral: can take with or without food.
Rinse mouth after inhaler use to prevent thrush.
Inhaler: rinse mouth after use. Oral: can take with or without food.
Take with food to reduce GI irritation. Morning dose preferred to mimic cortisol rhythm.
Take with food. Active form of prednisone—preferred in liver impairment.
Take with food to reduce stomach irritation. Dose packs: follow taper schedule.
Can be taken with or without food. May cause mild drowsiness.
Can be taken with or without food. Non-drowsy.
Take on empty stomach. Avoid fruit juices—they reduce absorption.
Causes significant drowsiness. Often used as sleep aid.
Take with food or milk. Long-acting—fewer doses needed.
Take with food to reduce GI effects. Available topical for local pain.
Can be taken with or without food. Lower GI risk than traditional NSAIDs.
Can be taken with or without food. Risk of serotonin syndrome with SSRIs.
ER forms: swallow whole. Take with food to reduce nausea.
Can be taken with or without food. ER: swallow whole.
Take with food to reduce nausea. Prodrug—requires CYP2D6 metabolism.
Take after meals with plenty of water. Stay well hydrated.
Can be taken with or without food. Narrow therapeutic index. Avoid grapefruit.
Take on empty stomach for best absorption. Take folic acid on non-MTX days.
Take with food or milk to reduce GI upset. Very long half-life.
Take with food. Drink plenty of water. May turn urine orange.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food. Drink plenty of water—kidney stone risk.
Take at onset of migraine. Avoid within 24h of ergot-type meds.
Take with food to enhance absorption. Do not stop abruptly.
Take on empty stomach. Food reduces absorption by 20%.
MUST take with food to slow absorption and reduce orthostatic hypotension.
Can be taken with or without food. ER: swallow whole.
Take with food to reduce GI upset. Avoid grapefruit.
Avoid grapefruit. ER: swallow whole. Do not use sublingual for hypertensive urgency.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food.
Take on empty stomach. High-fiber meals reduce absorption. Narrow therapeutic index.
Take with food to increase absorption. Extremely long half-life (40-55 days).
Can be taken any time but evening preferred. Not significantly affected by food.
Take in evening. Not affected by grapefruit (unlike other statins).
Take with food to increase absorption.
Can be taken with or without food.
Take 30 min after the same meal each day.
Can be taken with or without food. Women of childbearing age should not handle crushed tablets.
Take on empty stomach for fastest onset. High-fat meal delays absorption.
Can be taken with or without food. Long duration of action.
Take at bedtime to minimize GI side effects. Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food.
Take on empty stomach. Protein-rich meals compete for absorption. Take 30 min before meals.
Take with food to reduce nausea.
Take with food to reduce nausea.
IR: 30-45 min before meals. ER: swallow whole with breakfast.
Take in morning. Avoid vitamin C/acidic foods at time of dosing—reduces absorption.
Take with food to reduce GI upset.
Take in the morning. Food may delay but not reduce absorption.
Take with food. Highly variable absorption. Narrow therapeutic index. Many drug interactions.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food. Requires regular blood monitoring for agranulocytosis.
Can be taken with or without food.
Take with food to reduce GI effects.
Take consistently either with or without food. Food increases bioavailability.
Can be taken with or without food. Causes drowsiness.
Take at bedtime due to sedation. Can be taken with or without food.
Take with food. Do not stop abruptly—risk of withdrawal seizures.
Consistent food/fasting for each dose. Food increases Cmax 20%. Causes sedation.
Take at bedtime due to sedation. Avoid alcohol.
Take at bedtime. Active metabolite of amitriptyline.
Less sedating TCA. Can be taken in morning.
Low dose for insomnia. Higher doses for depression. Take at bedtime.
Take with food to reduce GI effects. Gold standard for OCD.
Avoid dairy, calcium, iron within 2 hours. Drink plenty of fluids.
Take with food to increase absorption and reduce GI upset.
Take with food and plenty of water. May cause photosensitivity.
Stearate form: take on empty stomach. Ethylsuccinate: take with food.
Take with food. Part of H. pylori triple therapy.
Take on empty stomach 1 hour before or 2 hours after meals.
Take at start of meal to reduce GI side effects and improve clavulanate absorption.
Take with food to increase absorption.
Can be taken with or without food.
Injectable. Do not mix with calcium-containing solutions.
Requires stomach acid for absorption. Take with food. Avoid PPIs.
Capsules: take with full meal for best absorption. Solution: take on empty stomach.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food. Drink plenty of water.
Can be taken with or without food. Prodrug of acyclovir with better bioavailability.
Take with food to reduce nausea. Start within 48h of symptom onset.
Can be taken with or without food. For pulmonary arterial hypertension.
Can be taken with or without food. Monthly liver function tests required.
Take on empty stomach with water. High-fat meal increases absorption variably.
Take with fatty meal to increase absorption significantly.
Take with fatty food to increase absorption. Chew or crush tablets.
Take on empty stomach 1 hour before meals. Take with pyridoxine (B6).
Take on empty stomach 1 hour before meals. Potent CYP inducer. May turn body fluids orange.
Take with food. Monitor uric acid levels.
Take with food. Monitor visual acuity regularly.
Eye drops. Apply nasolacrimal occlusion to reduce systemic absorption.
Apply once daily in the evening. May darken iris color over time.
Eye drops. Wait 15 min between different eye drops.
MUST take with high-fat meal to double absorption. Teratogenic—pregnancy prevention required.
Take with food. Monitor for methemoglobinemia and hemolytic anemia.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food. Do not stop abruptly—rebound hypertension.
Start at bedtime to minimize first-dose orthostatic hypotension.
Take at bedtime to minimize orthostatic hypotension.
Take at bedtime.
Swallow whole—do not open capsule. Very long half-life (5 weeks).
Can be taken with or without food. Swallow whole.
ER: swallow whole on empty stomach.
Take 30 minutes before meals.
Take 1 hour before meals on empty stomach. Separate from other meds by 2 hours.
Take with food. Used to prevent NSAID-induced ulcers. Contraindicated in pregnancy.
Take with food. Do not crush or chew delayed-release formulations.
Swallow whole. Avoid grapefruit juice.
Take with food to reduce nausea. Avoid allopurinol—dangerous interaction.
Take on empty stomach for best absorption. Myfortic (enteric-coated) can be with food.
Take on empty stomach. Avoid grapefruit. Narrow therapeutic index.
Take consistently with meals. Avoid grapefruit. Narrow therapeutic index.
Take consistently with or without food. Avoid grapefruit.
Can be taken with or without food.
Take within 15-30 minutes of each meal. Skip dose if skipping meal.
Take with first bite of each main meal.
Take before first meal of the day.
Can be taken with or without food. No renal dose adjustment needed.
Can be taken with or without food.
Sublingual: dissolve under tongue. Do NOT swallow. Sit down when taking.
Take in morning. ER: swallow whole.
Take with food to increase absorption.
Apply to dry scalp. Wash hands after application.
Take 1 hour before travel for motion sickness. Can cause drowsiness.
Take with food to reduce nausea. Highly sedating.
Take with food. Very potent—long-acting steroid.
Take with food. Monitor potassium and blood pressure.
Can be taken with or without food.
Can be taken with or without food. Preferred in first trimester pregnancy.
Oral: take with food. Patches: apply to clean, dry skin.
Take with food at bedtime. Causes drowsiness.
Take at same time every day. Mini-pill: strict timing required.
Take at same time every day. Vomiting/diarrhea may reduce effectiveness.
Oral: take with food. Injectable: every 3 months.
Gel: apply to shoulders/upper arms. Wash hands after. Avoid skin contact with others.
Take first thing in morning with full glass of water ONLY. Wait 30 min before food/other meds. Stay upright.
Take on empty stomach with water. Stay upright for 30 min.
Can be taken with or without food.
Nasal spray: alternate nostrils daily.
Weekly dosing. Take folic acid on other days. Empty stomach preferred.
Can be taken with or without food. Very long half-life (14 days).
Subcutaneous injection every 2 weeks. Rotate injection sites.
Subcutaneous injection. Keep refrigerated.
IV infusion. Given in clinical setting.
Take with food. Prodrug—less direct GI toxicity than other NSAIDs.
Take with food. Once-daily dosing. Preferential COX-2 selectivity.
Take with food. Very long half-life—once daily.
Take with food or antacids. High GI toxicity risk.
Short-term use only (≤5 days). Take with food. Very potent NSAID.
Can be taken with or without food.
Take with food. Drink plenty of water. Avoid aspirin.
Take with food to reduce GI effects. Very long half-life.
Take with food and full glass of water. Can cause vivid dreams.
Take with food or milky drink to maximize absorption.
Oral suspension: swish and swallow. Not systemically absorbed.
Dissolve slowly in mouth. Do not chew.
Take with fatty meal to greatly increase absorption. Avoid alcohol.
Take on empty stomach. More potent and faster acting than T4.
Take on empty stomach. Restrict fluid intake to avoid hyponatremia.
Take with food to reduce nausea. Twice weekly dosing.
Take with food to reduce GI side effects.
Subcutaneous injection. Schedule between meals to reduce GI effects.
Take with food. Swallow whole. Monitor levels.
Take at onset of migraine. Avoid with triptans. Sublingual dissolve.
MLT form dissolves on tongue without water.
Nasal spray or oral. Take at onset of migraine.
Can be taken with or without food. Sedating—bedtime preferred.
Take with food. Metabolized to phenobarbital.
Take with food to reduce GI upset.
Can be taken with or without food. Regular vision monitoring required.
Take with food to slow absorption and reduce CNS side effects.
Can be taken with or without food. Drink plenty of water—kidney stone risk.
Swallow whole. Avoid grapefruit. Food increases Cmax but not extent.

Can be taken with or without food. Monitor liver function.

Take consistently with or without food. High-fat meals reduce absorption.
Can be taken with or without food.

Can be taken with or without food. Avoid high-tyramine foods (aged cheese, red wine) as it acts as a reversible MAOI.
Take with or without food. Inhibits serotonin reuptake; risk of serotonin syndrome with SSRIs/MAOIs.
CNS stimulant; take in the morning to avoid insomnia. Avoid taking with caffeine or other stimulants.
Systemic vasoconstrictor; may elevate blood pressure.
Do not take with high-fat meals as it significantly increases absorption and risk of side effects (hypotension).
Extended-release; do not crush or chew. Take consistently at bedtime.
Standard therapeutic dose for depression/pain. Nausea is common on starting; take with food.
Take with food at the same time each day. Do not open capsules.
Maximum recommended dose. Can be taken with or without food.
Take with meals to prevent transient GI distress.
Activating SSRI; take in the morning.
Taking with food increases rate and extent of absorption. Highly lipophilic; avoid grapefruit.
Taking with food increases bioavailability by 90%. Avoid potassium supplements.
Take with breakfast to minimize nocturia and increase absorption.
Narrow therapeutic index. Fiber reduces absorption. Do not take with high-fiber meals.
Narrow therapeutic index. Monitor potassium and calcium levels.
Maintain strict consistent dietary vitamin K intake. Avoid new supplements without consulting doctor.
High risk of bleeding. Regular INR testing required.
Used for stroke prevention. Take twice daily with or without food.
Used for DVT/PE treatment. Take twice daily with or without food.
MUST take with a meal containing food to ensure adequate absorption.
MUST take with a meal containing food to ensure adequate absorption.
Avoid concurrent calcium, iron, or dairy products (separate by 2 hours before or 6 hours after).
Take with food if gastric irritation occurs. Avoid concurrent calcium, iron, or antacids.
Gastric irritant. Take with food or milk to prevent GI upset.
Prescription strength. Take with food. Do not combine with other NSAIDs.
Do not exceed 4000mg per day from all sources. Avoid alcohol due to liver toxicity risk.
Take on an empty stomach. High fat meals delay absorption.
Cialis maximum dose. Long action up to 36 hours.
Take with meals to reduce gastrointestinal side effects. Avoid excessive alcohol.
High dose statin. Avoid grapefruit juice entirely.
Potent statin. Antacids reduce absorption; separate by 2 hours.
Must take on an empty stomach with a full glass of water, 30-60 min before breakfast.
Food increases bioavailability. Avoid concurrent alcohol.
Extended release beta blocker. Take with or immediately after a meal.
Take with or without food. Complete the entire course of therapy.
Take with a full glass of water to avoid esophageal irritation. Can cause severe colitis.
Take at first sign of gout flare. Avoid grapefruit juice entirely.
Take after a meal with plenty of water. Monitor for hypersensitivity skin rashes.
Prodrug. Do not take with PPIs like omeprazole as they block conversion.
Administered sublingually under the tongue. Do not swallow. Avoid PDE5 inhibitors (Viagra).
Take in the evening. Monitor for neuropsychiatric changes.
Take consistently with or without food. Avoid potassium supplements.
Antihypertensive. Monitor kidney function and blood pressure.
Take at same time each day. Grapefruit juice can increase concentration slightly.
Maximum dose. Monitor for peripheral edema (swelling of ankles).
Take 30-60 minutes before the first meal of the day.
Take 30-60 minutes before breakfast. Reduces absorption of calcium, magnesium, B12.
Do not take within 2 hours of antacid supplements as they reduce absorption.
